steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a choice that emphasizes a difference between the two plants. The key difference in the notes is native status: Clermontia kakeana is native to Oahu, while Ardisia elliptica is non-native. The answer must put both plants together and directly contrast them on this point.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Seeds from Clermontia kakeana and Ardisia elliptica plants were found in the fecal samples of non-native Hawaiian birds, according to a 2019 study.': Mentions both plants but treats them the same — both had seeds in fecal samples. Highlights a similarity, not a difference. ✗
  • Choice B — 'A 2019 study catalogued plant seeds found in bird fecal samples in Oahu, Hawaii to determine what role non-native birds play in seed dispersal.': Describes the study's methodology without mentioning either plant by name. No contrast is made. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Most plant species found in Oahu, Hawaii, like Ardisia elliptica, are non-native.': Mentions only one plant and makes a general claim about Oahu's flora. Clermontia kakeana is absent, so no difference between the two plants is drawn. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Though Clermontia kakeana and Ardisia elliptica can both be found in Oahu, Hawaii, only the former plant is native.': Names both plants, establishes they share the same location, then directly contrasts their native status. This is a textbook contrast structure. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
